arts.ttl: No Fear: Shakhtar Donetsk not intimidated by Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Shakhtar Donetsk defender Mykola Matviyenko is going to have one of the hardest jobs on the pitch when his squad takes on Bayern Munich in the Champions League later today. Yes, we know that Bayern are probably one of the best teams in the world: they play very serious football, have very powerful players in every position.
Tom Bischof has insisted that he is not distracted by the speculation linking him with Bayern Munich, among other clubs.

arts.ttl: Alphonso Davies can sign Man Utd contract in January
arts.sum: Alphonso Davies will not sign a new Bayern Munich deal before the end of the year, meaning Manchester United will have a shot at signing the Canada superstar on a pre-contract in January. This means that Man Utd will legally have a shot at opening negotiations with Davies from January and convincing the left back to sign a pre-contract that would see him arrive on a Bosman Transfer in July.

arts.ttl: Karius linked with move to Bayern
arts.sum: Bild reports that Bayern Munich are considering signing a new goalkeeper in January, naming Loris Karius as a candidate.

arts.ttl: The rise of Bayern Munich star Jamal Musiala—Part 2: He only got his first phone because of football
arts.sum: Like most children during the age of technology, Bayern Munich ’s Jamal Musiala wanted a phone but his mum had misgivings about it. “See, I got my phone when I was 8 years old.

arts.ttl: Leroy Sané could leave Bayern Munich on a free transfer in the summer
arts.sum: Sky Germany reports that it can not be ruled out that Bayern Munich winger Leroy Sané leave the club on a free transfer in the summer. Sané’s contract at the club…

arts.ttl: Vincent Kompany responds to claim by Toni Kroos that Bayern Munich is too reliant on Jamal Musiala
arts.sum: In a recent episode of the Einfach mal Luppen podcast, former Bayern Munich, Real Madrid, and German national team midfielder Toni Kroos made a remark about how he felt Bayern was far too reliant on Jamal Musiala, especially against the stronger opponents they face. He might very well have already tallied a total of 10 goals and 4 assists from 18 appearances across all competitions and he has also scored twice and assist thrice for Germany in UEFA Nations League play from the last set of international breaks, but that is perhaps more of a call for other Bayern players needing to be more productive rather than a question of why the club is so reliant on him.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich contract updates: Jamal Musiala, Joshua Kimmich, and Alphonso Davies
arts.sum: Jamal Musiala, Joshua Kimmich and Alphonso Davies all have written contract offers from Bayern on the table. Three Bayern Munich players, Jamal Musiala, Joshua Kimmich, and Alphonso Davies, all have contracts that run out soon.

arts.ttl: Shakhtar Donetsk boss says no motivation is needed to face Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Of course, I will tell them a couple of things about how to play this game and what to do, but I think everyone is already highly motivated when playing in the Champions League, especially against such opponents,” said Pušić (as captured by @iMiaSanMia ). For Shakhtar Donetsk coach Marino Pušić, Bayern Munich is one of those rare teams that he does not have to think about whether his team will be “up” for the match.

arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Manchester City’s Pep Guardiola dreaming of double swoop for Bayern Munich’s Joshua Kimmich and Jamal Musiala; Erik ten Hag the key to keeping David Raum in Germany?; Valencia eyeing Mathys Tel; PSG to sell Xavi Simons?; and MORE!
arts.sum: Right now, it looks like Bayern Munich is going to ink Joshua Kimmich and Jamal Musiala to contract extensions that will keep them in Bavaria for a lot longer. Bayern Munich is looking to continue its winning form against Shakhtar Donetsk in the Champions League after a very odd 4-2 win over Heidenheim over the weekend.
